MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Nia Perkins, Esq. 510-835-3400 Toschi Collins & Doyle for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

Argued. Counsel apologizes for the late contest email. Counsel submits on the tentative ruling on the demurrer and contests the motion to strike only. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2- DEFENDANT DEMARRIS EVANS' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendant DeMarris Evans' motion to strike punitive damage allegations from the complaint is denied. Evans failed to file a responsive pleading by November 9, 2023, as required by court order. (10/12/23 Order.) For the pleading stage, the punitive damage allegations are adequate. For example, Evans allegedly had notice that the person he entrusted his car to had a history of driving while impaired by drugs and/or alcohol, yet did so knowing she would have to drive a substantial distance. (Cmplt. 6:18-7:4, 7:23-8:2.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. , Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Nia Perkins, Esq. 510-835-3400 Toschi Collins & Doyle for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

Argued. Counsel apologizes for the late contest email. Counsel submits on the tentative ruling on the demurrer and contests the motion to strike only. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1- DEFENDANT DEMARRIS EVANS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant DeMarris Evans' demurrer to the complaint is overruled. Evans failed to file a responsive pleading by November 9, 2023, as required by court order. (10/12/23 Order.) Negligence by Evans is adequately pled on an entrustment theory. (Cmplt. 6:18-23, 7:23-8:2.) Demurrer is not a proper means to attack a punitive damages prayer. (See Venice Town Council, Inc. v. City of Los Angeles (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 1547, 1562.)
For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. , Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
